question_text: Grass produces 10,000 kJ of food energy per year. Cows eat the grass and store 1,000 kJ as body tissue. Why is 90% of the energy lost and where does it go?
correct_answer: Energy is lost as heat during respiration, in waste products, and in uneaten parts of plants. Only about 10% can be stored as new body tissue
explanation: Organisms use most of the food energy they consume for movement, maintaining body temperature, and other life processes. Only a small fraction is converted into new body tissue that can be eaten by the next organism in the food chain.
